tweets.title:
RT by @sidhant: Every year, Ghallughara Day carries deep pain, grief and memory for the Sikh community. Today’s commemoration at Sri Akal Takht Sahib was observed peacefully, with dignity and religious maryada.

The appeal by the Jathedar of Sri Akal Takht Sahib (@J_SriAkalTakht ) for peaceful remembrance was important.

At the same time, this day also remains a serious challenge for the admin and security forces, especially Punjab Police. Their responsibility is not easy. They have to maintain peace while also respecting the emotions, pain and religious sentiments attached to this day.

In the past, we have seen that even a small incident outside Sri Harmandir Sahib has often been used by sections of national and international media to paint Punjab in a negative light. But when the commemoration passes peacefully, the story quietly disappears from the media space. There is no fuel for those who want to show Punjab only through the lens of disturbance.

That is why today’s peaceful observance deserves appreciation. The @SGPCAmritsar administration, Punjab Police, civil administration, religious leadership and sangat all played their role in ensuring that the remembrance was conducted with discipline, dignity and peace.

Pain should never be forgotten. History should never be erased. But remembrance with maryada, ardas and peace gives strength to the community and dignity to the memory of those we remember.

May Punjab always remain peaceful. May wisdom, restraint and compassion guide us all.

#OperationBlueStar

tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: The root of the conflict dates back to the 1816 Treaty of Sugauli between the British East India Company and Nepal. The treaty established the Kali River as the western boundary between the two nations. The problem? It never explicitly defined the geographic source of the river. However, since the 19th century, British India, and subsequently the Republic of India, has exercised continuous, uninterrupted administrative and revenue control over the Kalapani-Lipulekh area.

tweets.title:
BREAKING: Ministry of Defence has officially implemented the Delegation of Financial Powers to Defence Services (DFPDS-2026). This major policy overhaul grants enhanced financial authority for revenue-related procurement, decentralising a budget value exceeding ₹1.25 Lakh Crore annually.

- Operational Readiness: Grants field commanders direct financial autonomy to expedite decision-making and enhance immediate combat preparedness.

- Doubled Works Capital: Financial allocations for structural and infrastructure works projects are officially doubled to eliminate delays and ensure timely completion.

- Indigenisation & Innovation: Significantly expands financial ceilings for research and development to reduce reliance on foreign OEMs. This directly boosts domestic private players, including MSMEs and defense startups.

This marks a structural shift toward military decentralisation and self-reliance in defense manufacturing.
